ev.headline: Rivian R2 Delivery Date Confirmed, But Lowest-Price Models Will Have to Wait
ev.summary: Rivian reveals R2 electric SUV pricing and features with deliveries starting this spring, though more affordable models are coming later.

ev.headline: VinFast to Resume US EV Plant Construction Despite Growing Losses
ev.summary: Vietnamese EV maker VinFast announces it will restart its North Carolina factory construction this year while reporting widened quarterly losses.

ev.headline: Volkswagen Showcases Next-Generation ID Cross EV with Advanced Tech
ev.summary: VW's new ID Cross features an advanced platform with intelligent lighting, autonomous driving capabilities, and bidirectional charging.

ev.headline: Federal EV Safety Exit Law Proposed Following Colorado Deadly Crash
ev.summary: A new federal law proposal would require easy-access manual releases on electric cars after multiple deaths from trapped occupants.

ev.headline: EV Sales Crashed 41% in January as Incentive Removal Impacts Market
ev.summary: EV registrations fell dramatically following the September 2025 removal of the $7,500 federal tax credit, though some brands gained market share.

ev.headline: February EV Sales Reach 68,951 Units, Down 26.8% Year-Over-Year
ev.summary: Cox Automotive reports February EV sales with continued challenges despite month-over-month improvements and Chevrolet's strong surge.

ev.headline: BMW Targets 50% EV Sales by 2030 With Major Investment
ev.summary: BMW announces ambitious goal to achieve sales parity between electric and gas cars by 2030, with significant new battery plant investments.

ev.headline: China Develops Breakthrough EV Battery Achieving 1,000+ km Range
ev.summary: Chinese scientists announce lithium metal battery with energy density exceeding 700 Wh/kg, enabling vehicles to exceed 1,000 km range.

ev.headline: Ganfeng Lithium Begins Mass Production of Semi-Solid-State Batteries
ev.summary: World's largest lithium metal producer starts manufacturing semi-solid-state batteries with 650 Wh/kg energy density for major automakers.

ev.headline: Factorial All-Solid-State Battery Moves Closer to Production
ev.summary: US-based Factorial Energy partners with Philenergy to scale its Solstice solid-state platform with 80% higher energy density than lithium-ion.

ev.headline: FAW Installs Industry First Semi-Solid-State EV Battery With 1,000+ km Range
ev.summary: Chinese automaker FAW Group deploys first lithium-rich manganese semi-solid-state battery enabling over 1,000 km driving range.

ev.headline: MIT Report: Brutal Times for US Battery Industry as Investments Decline
ev.summary: US battery startups face major headwinds with declining investments and company failures, while China's battery sector thrives.

ev.headline: Sodium-Ion Batteries Emerging as Cheaper Alternative to Lithium
ev.summary: Sodium-ion batteries now cost less than average lithium-ion alternatives and are being deployed at scale in China.

energy.headline: Appeals court upholds California's rooftop solar rules
energy.summary: California First Appellate District Court of Appeals upheld NEM 3.0, the controversial net metering policy that reduced solar export compensation by 75%. The ruling keeps the policy in place despite legal challenges from environmental groups.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves and energy prices soar
energy.summary: As net metering policies become less favorable, residential battery storage is transforming from optional to essential for home solar economics. Integrated whole-home energy systems combining solar, batteries, EVs and smart panels are emerging as the new market standard.

energy.headline: US residential solar enters post-incentive era after ITC expiry surge
energy.summary: After the One Big Beautiful Bill Act eliminated the 30% federal tax credit for purchased solar systems on December 31, 2025, the industry enters a new phase where financing flexibility and integrated electrification will determine competitiveness. Third-party ownership models like leases and PPAs are gaining market share.

energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before the tax credit cut and reshaped the market
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar before the December 31, 2025 deadline, creating a 205% surge in demand. The rush reshaped contractor business models toward integrated home electrification, with battery attachment rates declining as homeowners prioritized securing solar incentives.

energy.headline: Expiring incentives led to record demand, more diverse market for residential solar in 2025
energy.summary: EnergySage's H2 2025 report shows the tax credit expiration drove a 205% increase in homeowner engagement and reshaped equipment choices, with contractors diversifying into integrated electrification services including heat pumps and EV charging.

energy.headline: California residents are increasingly pairing battery storage with solar installations
energy.summary: California data shows battery-paired solar installations jumped from 20% in October 2023 to over 50% by April 2024, driven by NEM 3.0's net billing tariff that incentivizes storage to capture peak-rate export opportunities.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries in 2026: Which Saves You More on Solar?
energy.summary: With net metering policies evolving across states and federal tax credits shifting, the decision between net metering and battery storage now depends heavily on location, utility compensation rates, and long-term energy goals rather than simple economics.

energy.headline: What Is NEM 3.0? Complete Guide To California's New Solar Policy
energy.summary: California's NEM 3.0 reduced solar export compensation by 75%, fundamentally changing how homeowners approach solar investments and making battery storage critical for system economics under the new net billing tariff structure.

energy.headline: Solar industry adapts after federal program cancellations
energy.summary: The solar industry is navigating market changes following the elimination of the Solar for All program and the 30% federal tax credit for residential installations. Industry experts report that consumers remain interested in solar for long-term savings despite the loss of incentives.

energy.headline: 2026-2027 U.S. Solar & HVAC Incentives State-by-State Analysis in a Post-Federal Credit Era
energy.summary: With federal 25D and 25C tax credits expiring, states are stepping up with localized incentive programs. California's SGIP program offers up to $1,100/kWh for battery storage, while Arizona, Texas, and other states introduce new state-level rebates for whole-home electrification.

energy.headline: Which States Offer the Best Incentives for Home Battery Systems in 2025
energy.summary: California, New York, Massachusetts, and Vermont lead with generous battery storage incentives ranging from $200-$1,100 per kWh. Programs combine upfront rebates, performance payments, and demand-response opportunities for homeowners installing battery systems.

v2x.headline: Tesla Cybertruck Gets Vehicle-to-Grid Capabilities with Powershare Grid Support
v2x.summary: Tesla launches Powershare Grid Support in Texas, enabling Cybertruck owners to send power back to the grid and earn bill credits through the first true V2G functionality.

v2x.headline: Yes, an EV really CAN power your home – if it's one of these
v2x.summary: Analysis of which EV models support V2H technology, highlighting GM's market leadership with 246,000+ equipped vehicles and new options from Tesla and other manufacturers.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Market Surges to USD 11.25 Billion by 2033
v2x.summary: Market report projects V2G market growth at 18.5% CAGR through 2033, with fleet-based grid services and energy optimization becoming highest-value applications.

v2x.headline: Xos Announces Production Rollout of Vehicle-to-Grid Capability
v2x.summary: Commercial EV manufacturer Xos launches V2G production in April 2026 on school bus platform, enabling fleets to return energy to grid and offset operating costs.

v2x.headline: Xos Launches V2G Technology for Commercial EV Fleets
v2x.summary: Los Angeles-based Xos begins V2G rollout in April 2026 starting with electric school buses, allowing fleets to generate revenue and reduce peak demand energy costs.

v2x.headline: Nissan to Launch Affordable Vehicle-to-Grid Technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announces plans to launch affordable bidirectional charging on selected EVs in 2026, leveraging experience from 40+ pilot projects and first-mover G99 Grid certification.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Technology Market Forecast for Rapid Expansion to US$ 70.9 Billion by 2031
v2x.summary: Market research shows V2G technology expanding from $2.9B in 2023 to $70.9B by 2031 with 49.4% CAGR, led by Europe and major automakers like Nissan and Mitsubishi.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging (V2G) in 2026: Which Cars Support It?
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide showing bidirectional charging reaching mainstream adoption in 2026 with major automakers including Ford, Tesla, GM, and others expanding V2L, V2H, and V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: GM Leads the Charge in Vehicle-to-Home EVs
v2x.summary: General Motors becomes market leader in V2H EVs with 246,000+ equipped vehicles sold, far exceeding competitors Ford and Tesla through Ultium platform-based lineup.

v2x.headline: These Are All The EVs That Can Power Your Home
v2x.summary: Detailed analysis of V2H-capable EVs currently available in the US market, including GM, Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla Cybertruck, Nissan Leaf, and Kia EV9 with respective charger requirements.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Home Charging Technology Launches for the Mass Market
v2x.summary: UC Irvine announces first mass-market V2H demonstration with six homes in California using commercial bidirectional chargers and Kia EV9 for both grid-tied and islanded power.

v2x.headline: GM Leads the Charge in Vehicle-to-Home EVs: Inside the Home Energy Ecosystem
v2x.summary: GM Energy explores leasing programs for home energy management systems and PowerBank batteries to make V2H more accessible as grid outages increase demand.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils New IQ Bidirectional EV Charger Architecture
v2x.summary: Enphase unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G capability, expected available in second half 2026, featuring AI-powered energy management and global standards compliance.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-2-Grid Conference 2026 in Münster, Germany
v2x.summary: International V2G, V2H, and Smart Charging conference scheduled April 15-16, 2026, bringing together automotive, energy, and technology leaders to discuss grid-connected mobility innovation.

v2x.headline: Which Electric Cars Have Bidirectional Charging?
v2x.summary: Updated guide showing 54 EV models with V2L capabilities and only 2 with official V2G/V2H support as of February 2026, including Cupra Born and vehicles with CHAdeMO connectors.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Chargers Explained: V2G, V2H & V2L in Australia
v2x.summary: Comprehensive Australian guide showing bidirectional charging now allowed under AS/NZS standards, with chargers ranging $5,000-$10,000, explaining V2G grid resilience and V2H home backup benefits.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging EV Chargers Review: V2G & V2H
v2x.summary: Technical review of bidirectional charger options including Wallbox Quasar, Sigenergy SigenStor, and emerging solutions, analyzing affordability and V2H/V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: What is V2H (Vehicle-to-Home)?
v2x.summary: Educational guide explaining V2H technology as emerging innovation where EVs function as mobile batteries, with pilots underway globally and commercial availability expanding.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Home (V2H): Hager Group and AUDI AG
v2x.summary: Hager Group and Audi partnership developing V2H bidirectional charging technology enabling electric vehicles to return energy back to homes as part of energy transition.

v2x.headline: V2H Technology: Powering the Future of Residential and Small Commercial Energy
v2x.summary: Analysis of V2H technology transformation showing GM, Ford, Tesla, and Kia expanding offerings with projections for increased compatibility, cost reduction, and utility collaboration.

v2x.headline: GM Makes Vehicle-to-Home Bidirectional Charging Available Across Ultium Portfolio
v2x.summary: General Motors announces V2H bidirectional charging expansion across Ultium-based EVs by model year 2026, with varied availability across vehicle categories and price points.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging EVs: V2G, V2H, And V2L Explained
v2x.summary: Educational explainer covering V2G grid services, V2H home backup power, and V2L device charging, with guidance on selecting EVs and noting V2G requires smart grid support.

v2x.headline: What Cars Support V2G? A Guide to Bidirectional Charging EVs
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide clarifying V2L, V2H, and V2G distinctions with system requirements and compatibility for Ford, GM Energy, Tesla Powershare, and upcoming models.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charger Guide: V2G/V2H for Fleet Profit & Savings
v2x.summary: Business-focused guide explaining V2G peak demand reduction, V2H backup power, V2L direct appliance power, and V2X overall term with fleet energy management applications.

v2x.headline: Nuvve Holding Corp. Press Releases
v2x.summary: Global V2G technology leader Nuvve announces multiple battery deployments, aggregation agreements, and partnerships expanding distributed grid assets management across markets.
